feedback.text: This is great! There is a lot of shady practices in the online market, especially regarding digital products such as software, games and services. I would love to see some points added to the DFA. Specifically regarding video games. Video games are now being "sold" to consumers without any demands on the developer to actually have an "end of life" plan at all. This means that people can "buy" games for 40-100 and the game can be removed from them at any point for any reason, without compensation or alternative solution. There should be a minimum time that the game can be accessed, clearly shown when purchasing the game, as well as a clear plan of what will happen when that time is reached. Another point that should be looked at is the "lifetime" version of a software. If a license to a software product is sold as "lifetime", it should be "lifetime". I understand that updates may not always be provided forever as that is not sustainable for the company, but I should always be able to download the software that I payed for.
